name: Flipkart Product Cache — L1 + L2 Performance Optimization
scenario: Flipkart's product page hits the DB 200M times per day. Add a two-level cache: Caffeine L1 (in-process, fast) + Redis L2 (distributed, shared). Target: 95% of requests served from cache, 5% hitting DB.

core.description: TwoLevelProductCache @Service: L1 Caffeine (1000, 30s, recordStats) + mock L2. get() checks L1 → L2 → DB. put() populates both.
Cache metrics: Counter by level (cache.hits{level=l1/l2/db}). Gauge for hit rate and cache size. All registered in constructor.
CacheWarmupService @EventListener: load top 100 products into Caffeine on startup. Log warm-up time.
@SpringBootTest (3 tests): second access returns from L1 (verify(repo, times(1))), hit rate > 0.5 after 2 hits + 1 miss, warm-up count >= 100. All 3 pass.
README: cache hit/miss sequence diagram (T=0, T=10s, T=35s, T=6min).

bonus.description: Stampede protection: StampedeProtectedGet using a ConcurrentHashMap<String,CompletableFuture> to coalesce duplicate in-flight requests. Test: 10 concurrent gets → only 1 DB call.
Write-through: updateProduct() writes to DB AND L1 simultaneously. Test: update reflects in get() without DB hit.
Prometheus alert rule: L2 hit rate < 80% for 10 minutes. alertmanager.yml routing.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Caffeine recordStats(): REQUIRED for cache.stats().hitRate() to work; without it, hitRate() always returns NaN. L1 TTL shorter than L2: L1 is a local buffer — short TTL ensures L1 re-fetches from L2 (which may have been updated) rather than serving stale data. Cache-aside delete before write: delete from cache BEFORE writing to DB (prevents window where concurrent read caches old value). Caffeine cache.get(key, loader): alternative to getIfPresent — calls loader on miss and caches result automatically. Counter by 'level' tag: enables PromQL filtering by level (l1/l2/db) without separate metric names.
